name: neg_1_always_err
property: from_raw_parts always returns Err(InvalidArgument) regardless of input validity
reason: An implementation that always returns Err is useless but satisfies the spec since the Ok branch is vacuously true and the Err branch only requires the error code.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: neg_1_always_err_valid_inputs
property: from_raw_parts always returns Err(InvalidArgument) regardless of input validity
reason: Non-null addr, large len, valid block_size that divides len — should succeed, but spec permits Err.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: neg_1_always_err_many_blocks
property: from_raw_parts always returns Err(InvalidArgument) regardless of input validity
reason: A 64KB region with 64B blocks should yield ~1024 blocks, but the spec still permits returning Err.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: neg_2_block_size_zero
property: from_raw_parts accepts block_size == 0 and returns Ok with a slab whose block_size is 0
reason: block_size == 0 makes modulo operations in allocate undefined; SlabView::inv() requires block_size > 0, so this should be rejected — tests that inv() catches it.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: neg_2_block_size_one
property: from_raw_parts accepts block_size == 1 losing alignment guarantees
reason: block_size == 1 means every address is trivially aligned (x % 1 == 0), eliminating any meaningful alignment guarantee from allocate.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: neg_2_block_size_exceeds_len
property: from_raw_parts accepts block_size > len where no blocks can fit
reason: When block_size > len, alignment constraints in inv() make it impossible to find valid start_addr/end_addr within the memory region, so the spec should reject this.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: neg_3_empty_free_addrs
property: from_raw_parts does not constrain free_addrs in its postcondition, so a newly created Slab can have an empty free_addrs set
reason: A slab born with zero free blocks is immediately full and useless, but SlabView::inv() only constrains membership, not non-emptiness.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: neg_3_empty_free_addrs_single_block
property: from_raw_parts does not constrain free_addrs when exactly one block should exist
reason: With len == 2 * block_size, at least one data block should be free, but the postcondition never requires free_addrs to reflect the available address range.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: neg_3_empty_free_addrs_inv_unconstrained
property: inv() does not relate free_addrs to the address range [start_addr, end_addr)
reason: SlabView::inv() never requires free_addrs ∪ allocated_addrs to cover all aligned addresses in [start_addr, end_addr); blocks can silently vanish.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: neg_4_free_addrs_below_region
property: from_raw_parts allows free_addrs to contain addresses outside the memory region [addr, addr+len)
reason: Address 0x800 < start_addr 0x1000 violates inv()'s containment check; tests whether inv() prevents below-region addresses in free_addrs.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: neg_4_free_addrs_above_region
property: from_raw_parts allows free_addrs to contain addresses beyond the memory region
reason: Address 0x3000 >= end_addr 0x2000 violates inv()'s range check; tests whether inv() prevents above-region addresses.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: neg_4_free_addrs_unaligned
property: from_raw_parts allows free_addrs to contain unaligned addresses within the region
reason: Address 0x1001 is within the region but not block_size-aligned; tests whether inv()'s alignment check on free_addrs catches this.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: neg_5_same_addr_single
property: allocate always returns the same address from free_addrs, never distributing across available blocks
reason: With only one free block, the choice is forced; this scenario is trivially correct and harmless.
params: pre: Slab, post: Slab, result: Result<*mut u8, Error>

name: neg_5_same_addr_multiple
property: allocate always returns the same address from free_addrs, never distributing across available blocks
reason: Three free blocks exist but spec permits always returning 0x1000; an adversarial implementation could starve other blocks indefinitely.
params: pre: Slab, post: Slab, result: Result<*mut u8, Error>

name: neg_5_same_addr_after_dealloc
property: allocate always returns the same address from free_addrs after alloc/dealloc cycles
reason: After alloc-dealloc-alloc, the spec allows re-returning 0x1000 every time while 0x1080 is never allocated; no fairness or coverage property exists.
params: pre: Slab, post: Slab, result: Result<*mut u8, Error>

name: phi_gap_1
property: On success, free_addrs is non-empty (at least one block is available for allocation). The spec only states allocated_addrs == Set::empty() but says nothing about free_addrs being non-empty.
reason: A successfully constructed slab with zero free blocks is useless — callers cannot prove any subsequent allocate() will succeed.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: phi_gap_1_minimal
property: On success, free_addrs is non-empty. When len == block_size * 2, exactly one data block exists so free_addrs should have exactly one element.
reason: With minimal valid input the body guarantees exactly one free block; the spec allows zero.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: phi_gap_1_large
property: On success, free_addrs is non-empty. When len is large relative to block_size, many data blocks exist so free_addrs should have many elements.
reason: A large memory region should yield many allocatable blocks; the spec permits none.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: phi_gap_2
property: Completeness of address partitioning: every block-aligned address in [start_addr, end_addr) belongs to either allocated_addrs or free_addrs. The spec allows addresses to be in neither set.
reason: Block-aligned addresses in [start_addr, end_addr) that are in neither set break capacity reasoning and conservation-of-blocks invariants.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: phi_gap_2_construction
property: After construction when allocated_addrs == empty(), the gap prevents proving free_addrs == all valid addresses. A missing address cannot be accounted for.
reason: With allocated_addrs empty and free_addrs empty, all block addresses are unaccounted for — callers cannot prove any block exists.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: phi_gap_2_partial
property: The gap prevents proving the number of remaining free blocks equals (total - allocated). At construction, total blocks minus zero allocated should yield all free, but the spec does not enforce this.
reason: The number of tracked blocks (|free| + |alloc| = 2) does not equal the expected total (7), breaking capacity reasoning.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: phi_gap_3
property: start_addr is strictly greater than addr (not merely >=). The body reserves at least one index block, so start_addr >= addr + block_size > addr. The spec only says start_addr >= addr.
reason: If start_addr == addr, the first data block overlaps the index bitmap region, corrupting metadata on allocation.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: phi_gap_3_minimal
property: When block_size is large relative to len (just fitting 2 blocks), num_index_blocks == 1 so start_addr == addr + block_size > addr. The spec allows start_addr == addr.
reason: With large block_size, the index block takes significant space; start_addr == addr would overlap index and data.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: phi_gap_3_small_block
property: When block_size is small relative to len, num_index_blocks could be > 1, making start_addr even further from addr. The spec still allows start_addr == addr.
reason: With small block_size, multiple index blocks are needed; start_addr == addr is even more dangerous as it overlaps more index space.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: phi_gap_4
property: On success, free_addrs contains ALL block-aligned addresses in [start_addr, end_addr). Since allocated_addrs is empty, every data block should be free. The spec does not characterize free_addrs contents.
reason: A caller cannot determine slab capacity or prove specific addresses are allocatable without knowing free_addrs's exact contents.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: phi_gap_4_minimal
property: With minimal len (2 * block_size), free_addrs should have exactly 1 element equal to start_addr. The spec allows free_addrs to be empty.
reason: The only data block address is missing from free_addrs, making the slab appear to have zero capacity.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: phi_gap_4_many_blocks
property: When len allows many data blocks, free_addrs should contain all of them. The spec allows free_addrs to be a strict subset.
reason: 5 out of 7 data block addresses are missing from free_addrs, silently reducing usable slab capacity.
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: phi_gap_5
property: On error, the error code is specifically ErrorCode::BadAddress. The spec uses Err(_) with a wildcard, discarding the error code entirely.
reason: Callers cannot distinguish error causes or rely on specific error codes for recovery logic when the spec uses a wildcard.
params: pre: Slab, post: Slab, ptr: *const u8, result: Result<(), Error>

name: phi_gap_5_below_range
property: When ptr is below start_addr (out of range), the body returns ErrorCode::BadAddress but the spec allows any error.
reason: Out-of-bounds-below should report BadAddress, but the spec permits InvalidArgument or any other code.
params: pre: Slab, post: Slab, ptr: *const u8, result: Result<(), Error>

name: phi_gap_5_above_range
property: When ptr is above end_addr (out of range), the body returns ErrorCode::BadAddress but the spec allows any error.
reason: Out-of-bounds-above should report BadAddress, but the spec permits any error code.
params: pre: Slab, post: Slab, ptr: *const u8, result: Result<(), Error>

name: phi_gap_5_unaligned
property: When ptr is in range but not block-aligned, the body returns ErrorCode::BadAddress but the spec allows any error.
reason: Unaligned-pointer errors should report BadAddress, but the spec permits any error code — callers cannot distinguish misalignment from other failures.
params: pre: Slab, post: Slab, ptr: *const u8, result: Result<(), Error>

name: phi_gap_5_already_free
property: When ptr is valid and block-aligned but the block is already free (double-free), the body returns ErrorCode::BadAddress but the spec allows any error.
reason: Double-free should report BadAddress, but the spec's wildcard Err(_) permits any code — callers cannot distinguish double-free from invalid-pointer errors.
params: pre: Slab, post: Slab, ptr: *const u8, result: Result<(), Error>

name: gap_6_unspecified_err_code
property: The spec uses Err(_) wildcard, accepting any error code on allocation failure. Callers cannot distinguish 'slab exhausted' (OutOfMemory) from other errors.
reason: Callers cannot programmatically distinguish slab-exhausted from other failures if any ErrorCode is accepted
params: pre: Slab, post: Slab, result: Result<*mut u8, Error>

name: gap_6_unspecified_err_code_nonempty
property: When free_addrs is non-empty (Ok path), the returned address should lie within [start_addr, end_addr). Testing if an out-of-range address can be returned.
reason: If spec accepted an out-of-range pointer, callers would access memory outside the slab; expects REJECT since free_addrs.contains(addr) + inv() prevents this
params: pre: Slab, post: Slab, result: Result<*mut u8, Error>

name: gap_6_unspecified_err_code_empty
property: When free_addrs is empty (Err path), the error code returned is unspecified by the spec due to the Err(_) wildcard pattern.
reason: With exhausted slab, returning BadAddress instead of OutOfMemory misleads callers about the failure cause; spec accepts it due to Err(_)
params: pre: Slab, post: Slab, result: Result<*mut u8, Error>

name: gap_6_unspecified_err_code_last_block
property: When exactly one free block remains (boundary), after it is allocated the slab is exhausted. The next allocation fails with an unspecified error code.
reason: At the boundary (slab just exhausted), returning IoErr instead of OutOfMemory is especially confusing for fallback allocation strategies
params: pre: Slab, post: Slab, result: Result<*mut u8, Error>

name: gap_7_weak_end_addr_bound
property: end_addr == addr + (len / block_size) * block_size exactly, but the spec only says end_addr <= addr + len. The weak bound allows wasting arbitrarily many complete blocks.
reason: Spec allows wasting 62 out of ~63 usable blocks; callers cannot reason about memory utilization from the spec alone
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: gap_7_weak_end_addr_bound_exact_multiple
property: When len is an exact multiple of block_size, end_addr should equal addr + len (no waste), but the spec allows end_addr < addr + len, wasting complete blocks.
reason: When len divides block_size evenly, zero waste is achievable; spec accepting smaller end_addr means callers cannot trust maximal utilization
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: gap_7_weak_end_addr_bound_remainder
property: When len % block_size != 0, end_addr should equal addr + len - (len % block_size). The spec allows end_addr to be even smaller than this truncated value, wasting additional complete blocks.
reason: Spec allows losing complete blocks beyond the unavoidable remainder waste; callers cannot prove the slab wastes less than block_size bytes
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>

name: gap_7_weak_end_addr_bound_large_block
property: When block_size is large relative to len, the gap between end_addr and addr + len can approach block_size bytes. The weak spec bound allows end_addr to be even smaller than the tight truncated value.
reason: With large block_size, each wasted block is substantial; spec allows losing 1024+ bytes that the body implementation would correctly utilize
params: addr: *mut u8, len: usize, block_size: usize, result: Result<Slab, Error>
